title: Field Food Stories and Hacks
gist: A Marine Pulse podcast episode description covers the fieldcraft, informal trading economy, and improvised meals built around the MRE (Meal, Ready-to-Eat) during a Marine infantry squad's deployment rotation.

insights.claim: Marine Corps infantryman Sgt. Chris Brown, who enlisted in 2007 and deployed to Afghanistan, discusses the fieldcraft of the MRE (Meal, Ready-to-Eat) and the informal trading system it creates among Marines in the field.
insights.speaker: War on the Rocks
insights.so_what: Illustrates how standardized military rations spawn an emergent barter economy among small units.

insights.claim: Care packages and improvised meals, beyond the official MRE supply, played a meaningful role in sustaining a Marine squad through a long deployment rotation.
insights.speaker: War on the Rocks
insights.so_what: Highlights the role of informal, non-official logistics in sustaining troop morale on long deployments.
insights.locator: the care packages and improvised meals that carried a squad through a long rotation
